evals.prompt: Set up a password-protected Obsidian Web vault on my Zo. I do not already have a vault and I did not provide a password.
evals.expected_output: Uses the zocomputer-obsidian password-protected workflow, creates a fresh safe vault, derives username from Zo context, generates a strong password via CLI, starts the service, and verifies a 302 login redirect.

evals.prompt: Set up a public readonly Obsidian Web vault on Zo. I want it public but I did not give you a vault path.
evals.expected_output: Uses the public-readonly workflow, creates a new public starter vault instead of scanning personal vaults, generates credentials if needed, starts the service, and verifies unauthenticated readonly behavior.

evals.prompt: Run obsidian-web on my Zo using /home/workspace/notes as my private vault. Generate a strong password for me.
evals.expected_output: Uses the provided vault path, does not ask unnecessary questions, generates a CLI password, configures AUTH_MODE=full, starts the service, and reports path, username, password, URL if available, and status code.
